sketchMechanisms: The `object` declaration creates a singleton — exactly one instance can ever exist, and it is initialized lazily the first time it is accessed.
The `never` return type indicates a function that can never successfully return — it must throw or loop forever.
Rust's ownership system: calling `drop()` on a value consumes it, and any subsequent attempt to use it is a compile-time error — the value is gone from scope forever.
